As per SAP Note 3710781 - Action Logs retention period in Landscape Manager for SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: AI-Assisted Commodity Work Center: For Smarter Commodity Trading
entry.content:
Commodity trading has never been more complex or more time sensitive. Global energy and agriculture markets move at extraordinary speed, shaped by volatile prices, regulatory shifts, logistics constraints, and constant streams of data. In this environment, traders don’t just need more information—they need intelligent support that helps them act faster and with confidence. 

That’s where AI Assisted Commodity Work Center (ACW) comes in. 

ACW is purpose-built to empower commodity traders with intelligent automation, real-time interaction, and seamless collaboration, all designed to match the pace and complexity of modern trading operations. 

 

Automate What Matters Most 

Traders should spend their time making decisions, not formatting documents or chasing repetitive tasks. 

ACW introduces AI agents that streamline and accelerate document creation directly through BTP to SAP S/4HANA by processing trade summaries written in natural language using Large Language Models. From trade summaries to the creation of draft commodity deals and contracts, ACW automates data capture tasks that traditionally slow traders down. 

By eliminating manual steps and reducing rework: 

  • Uses natural language to capture and process trade summaries from various sources—such as chats, emails, meeting transcripts, and more—so traders don’t need to follow specific trade summary formats 
  • Examples: 
    • Purchase 3 Ships (28,000 barrels 60F / ship) ULSD Diesel from Oil Company, one lifting per month for JFM 2026 (3 total lifts), priced at the Monthly Average Chicago OPIS Index plus $0.05. FOB Chicago Ship. 
    • Purchase Golden Harvest Inc 1000 MT Soybeans Grade A CIF Baton Rouge ZSK2026 Jan - March by ship 
  • Traders reclaim valuable time during peak trading hours 
  • Errors caused by manual data capture are significantly reduced 
  • Operational efficiency improves without disrupting existing systems 

The result? Traders stay focused on strategy and execution, while ACW handles the administrative elements of deal capture.  

 

Capture deals with confidence 

In commodity markets, timing is everything. Delayed decisions can mean missed opportunities, or increased risk. 

ACW delivers real-time AI driven data capture that allows traders to instantly convert trade summaries into actionable documents. Instead of switching between systems or waiting on downstream processes, traders can move seamlessly from insight to execution. 

With ACW: 

  • Critical information is surfaced and validated when it’s needed most 
  • Trade-related data is contextualized, not just displayed 
  • Data capture becomes faster, more accurate, and more consistent across multiple deal types 

Progress Bar

With error indicator Indicating warnings to be reviewed by the user Ready to create the document 

This means that traders can replace their traditional handwritten deal sheets/trade books by writing the same trade summary into ACW and have the trade created in the system. 

 

Scale with intelligence 

As organizations grow, so do their trading teams. But scaling expertise has traditionally been difficult; new traders face steep learning curves, complex systems, and fragmented workflows. ACW changes that dynamic. 

With an intuitive, trader-centric user interface, ACW enables rapid deal capture for every trader, regardless of experience level. The platform is designed to feel natural, reducing onboarding time and helping teams become productive faster. 

This means: 

  • Faster ramp-up for new traders 
  • More consistent execution across teams 
  • Institutional knowledge embedded into everyday workflows 

Message Bar

Easy-to-understand warning and error messages helping traders to identify what needs to be done. 

 

Built for the reality of commodity markets 

ACW is not a generic AI tool, it’s designed specifically for commodity trading organizations, where precision, speed, and compliance matter. By integrating directly into existing enterprise environments and aligning with real trader workflows, ACW enhances how teams work without forcing them to work differently. 

 

Conclusion - The Future of Trading Starts Here 

ACW represents a new standard for commodity trading operations—one where AI works alongside traders, not instead of them. By automating what matters most, delivering real-time AI driven data capture, and scaling intelligence across teams, ACW enables traders to operate with greater speed, clarity, and confidence. 

In markets where every decision counts, ACW is the AI Work Center that keeps traders ahead. 

SAP Help Documentation 

  • Admin Guide: Overview | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: LGM Knowledge Snippet: Automated (BN4L-ID based) vs Manual Subcontracting
entry.content:
When you work with subcontracting in SAP Logistics Management, you might wonder: how does the system know whether to wait for an automated carrier response or require manual confirmation/rejection? The answer lies in a simple but crucial piece of business partner data: the BN4L ID.

What Is BN4L?

BN4L stands for Business Network for Logistics — SAP’s cloud-based collaboration platform that connects shippers with carriers. Through BN4L, SAP Logistics Management can send subcontracting requests to carriers and receive their responses — all automatically, without any manual actions.

However, not every carrier is connected to BN4L. Some may use traditional communication channels, such as phone or email, to confirm their transportation orders. SAP Logistics Management must determine which carriers can communicate via BN4L and which require manual handling.

The BN4L ID: Your Carrier’s Digital Fingerprint

The key factor in deciding whether a carrier is connected to BN4L is the BN4L ID (also known as the LBN ID), maintained as an external identifier on the carrier’s business partner data. When a carrier is onboarded to SAP Business Network for Logistics, they receive this unique identifier, which enables electronic communication.

In SAP S/4HANA, this ID is maintained in the business partner master data under the Identification section with the identification type LBN001 (SAP Business Network for Logistics ID).

Subcontracting In SAP Logistics Management

So, in SAP Logistics Management, the BN4L ID is used for subcontracting mode determination and serves as the primary identification for automated communication with carriers via BN4L. To enable this communication, the BN4L integration must be properly configured. This understanding sets the stage for a detailed look at the subcontracting process in LGM.

Subcontracting Mode Determination

The process starts when the Send to Carrier action is triggered for a consignment. SAP Logistics Management checks the carrier’s business partner identification data for the BN4L ID to determine how confirmation should be handled:

  • If the carrier has a BN4L ID. The system expects an electronic confirmation via SAP Business Network for Logistics, and no manual action is required.
  • If the carrier does not have a BN4L ID. The system expects manual confirmation/rejection after contacting the carrier outside the system.

This BN4L-based mechanism is particularly relevant in the Direct LTL shipping process, where the transportation order is sent for subcontracting after the tendering process

Identifying BN4L Carriers On The UI

In the Manage Consignments app, you can add the Carrier column to your list report view. This indicator shows whether the assigned carrier has a maintained BN4L ID, helping you quickly identify which consignments will receive automated responses and which require manual handling.

Subcontracting Process Flow

  • For Non-BN4L Carriers (Manual Process)

The consignment Carrier Confirmation Status is set to Confirmation Pending, and the system marks the consignment with Action Required (reason: Open Task) to highlight this manual step.

At the end of the subcontracting process, a manual action must be triggered to update the consignment Carrier Confirmation Status to Confirmed or Rejected.

  • For BN4L Carriers (Automated Process)

The Carrier Confirmation Status updates to Confirmation Pending, with additional information indicating that the system is waiting for a response from the carrier via SAP Business Network for Logistics.

The consignment Carrier Confirmation Status automatically updates to Confirmed or Rejected based on the carrier response.

Setting Up BN4L Integration In SAP Logistics Management

To enable BN4L subcontracting, the integration must be configured using the following steps:

  • Maintain the BN4L ID in S/4HANA. Add an identification of type LBN001 to the carrier’s Business Partner master data in SAP S/4HANA. The LBN ID must be retrieved from the SAP Business Network for Logistics and also set on the master data record.
  • Replicate the Business Partners to SAP Logistics Management. Once the carrier data is updated, replicate the Business Partner to SAP Logistics Management using the Business Partner Replication process from the connected S/4 system. Replication can run automatically via a scheduled job or ad-hoc.
  • Configure the BN4L Connection. Set up the technical connection to BN4L by maintaining the communication system, creating the communication arrangement, and configuring the destination with the correct authentication details. Follow the BN4L Integration Setup guide to ensure successful communication between SAP Logistics Management and BN4L.

Wrapping Up

In SAP Logistics Management, the subcontracting confirmation mode is determined by verifying whether a BN4L ID is assigned to the carrier — its presence enables automated electronic confirmations, while its absence requires manual confirmation/rejection.

This approach makes managing subcontracting confirmations simple and clear:

  • Single source of truth. The BN4L ID on the carrier master data shows whether a carrier can use BN4L. You don’t need extra flags or settings.
  • Automatic updates. When a carrier connects to BN4L and you replicate their data, their consignments automatically switch to electronic confirmation.
  • Works with all carriers. BN4L carriers get automated confirmations, while carriers not connected to BN4L are handled manually. You can manage both types in the same system without issues.

entry.title: Building a Custom Login Page for SAP Commerce Composable Storefront( JDK21 , OAUTH2)
entry.content:
Why Build a Custom Login Page?

A custom login page enables:

  • Consistent brand experience

  • Embedded login within CMS-driven pages

  • Better UX and higher conversion

  • Enterprise-specific authentication extensions

  • Compliance with Authorization Code Flow

With the SAP 2211JDk21  Authorization Server update, custom login pages are supported when implemented correctly.

High-Level Architecture

The storefront hosts the custom login UI, but authentication is handled by the Authorization Server.

I followed the help doc for custom Help Custom Login Page  login page and created below flow

The browser must preserve the same session (JSESSIONID) across:

  1. GET /authorizationserver/oauth/authorize

  2. GET /authorizationserver/csrf

  3. POST /authorizationserver/login

If the session changes, CSRF validation fails.

End-to-End Flow Summary

1️⃣User accesses a protected page (e.g., /my-account)
2️⃣Storefront redirects to /oauth/authorize
3️⃣Authorization Server redirects to custom /login page
4️⃣Storefront fetches CSRF token
5️⃣User submits credentials
6️⃣Authorization Server resumes flow and returns authorization code
7️⃣Storefront exchanges code for access + refresh tokens
8️⃣Storefront calls OCC APIs with access token
9️⃣Authenticated page is rendered

This ensures:

  • No access token exposed in URL

  • Secure token exchange

  • CSRF-protected login

  • Full OAuth2 compliance

 Implementation Overview

Step 1 – Create Custom Login Component

Replace ReturningCustomerLoginComponent with a custom component that:

  • Uses LoginFormComponentService

  • Retrieves CSRF token

  • Posts to /authorizationserver/login

  • Maintains session continuity

UI is fully customizable while preserving the secure flow.

Step 2 – CMS Mapping

Use provideConfig to map:

 
ReturningCustomerLoginComponent → CustomLoginComponent

This ensures Spartacus renders your UI while keeping CMS routing intact.

Step 3 – Configure OAuth Endpoints

Update AuthConfig:

 
provideConfig(<AuthConfig>{
    authentication: {
      baseUrl: 'https://localhost:9002/authorizationserver',
      loginUrl: '/oauth/authorize',
      tokenEndpoint: '/oauth/token',
      revokeEndpoint: '/oauth/revoke',
      logoutUrl: '/logout',
      userinfoEndpoint: '/oauth/userinfo',
      sendAuthHeaderOnRevoke: false,
      useClientTokens: false,
      customLoginPage: {
        csrfEndpoint: '/csrf',
        loginFormEndpoint: '/login'
      }
    }
 

This config drives the full OAuth2 orchestration.

Step 4 – OAuth Client Configuration

In Backoffice or via ImpEx:

  • Set loginPageUri

    • Dev → http://localhost:4200/login

    • Prod → https://storefront.example.cloud/login

  • Configure allowed hosts on local.properties :

    • authserver.oauthclientdetails.loginpageuri.allowed.hosts

    • corsfilter.authorizationserver.allowedOrigins

⚠️The login page must be hosted on:

  • The same domain, OR

  • A sub-domain of the authorization server

authserver.oauthclientdetails.loginpageuri.allowed.hosts=http://localhost:4200,http://localhost:9001,https://localhost:9002
corsfilter.authorizationservices.allowedOrigins=http://localhost:4200
corsfilter.authorizationservices.allowedMethods=GET,OPTIONS,POST
corsfilter.authorizationservices.allowCredentials=true

Validation Checklist

Custom Login page will look like this :

✔ Login renders at /login
✔ CSRF request uses same session
✔ Login POST succeeds
✔ Authorization code returned
✔ Token exchange works
✔ Logout works
✔ Guest checkout remains unaffected

Security Best Practices

  • Keep authorizationCodeFlowByDefault enabled

  • Use withCredentials: true for cross-subdomain setups

  • Do not break session boundaries

  • Always validate allowed host configuration

Enterprise Benefits

This pattern enables:

  • Fully branded authentication experience

  • Embedded login capabilities

  • Secure OAuth2 compliance

  • Compatibility with SAP Commerce Cloud 2211+

  • Production-ready Composable Storefront architecture

For organizations modernizing their SAP Commerce stack, this approach balances security, flexibility, and user experience.

If you’re working on:

  • OAuth2 customization

  • CCv2 storefront modernization

  • SAP Commerce 2211+ upgrades

  • JDK 21 migrations with updated Authorization Server

Happy to connect and discuss architecture strategies.

I have attached the custom login component : Custom Components

entry.title: Re: Understanding the Evolution of SAP SuccessFactors BizX Architecture
entry.content:
The evolution described above is due to the following:

  • Before being acquired by SAP in 2012, SuccessFactors had acquired five (5) major companies along with several smaller ones. After the SAP acquisition, Employee Central was introduced as the unified master‑data solution.

Because these modules originated from different companies, SAP SuccessFactors has worked extensively to integrate these heterogeneous systems. After multiple rounds of refactoring and significant effort, they have now been successfully brought together into a more unified platform.

entry.title: ABAP RAP Query - IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER_Why there is no GET_DATA method in IO_REQUEST?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

IO_REQUEST parameter of Select method has several methods that can get filters, parameters, paging etc. but why it don't have any get_data that carries the structure/internal table of the CDS entity like how set_data is available for IO_RESPONSE? 

We have a requirement where we receive a payload file in JSON format with some fields with values filled from source system that will be used to fetch data from SAP and send back the results in same JSON structure. while input and output structure is same, we couldn't utilize IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER~Select method as the data from JSON cannot be read through GET. So we had to use POST method to perform data fetch operation from SAP and write back the JSON in RESULTS set via behavior implementation CREATE method. 

Any clarity on this would be helpful for knowledge and learning purpose. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: In Datasphere Analytic Model, can I have two structur...
entry.content:
AM is not only like BW Query.  AM is a combination of a HCPR with ADSO features and Query. There are limitations to the structures, such as using only base-measures (Model-VIEW/FACT-Level). So you need to create the baseline up-front in the View (stacked AMs not supported). For the current/previous FY offsets can be used via derived variables.

You can create structures and use them separately or in calculated structures

You simulate the rows / columns in the SAC Story structure. 

Here a quick&dirty example: 

Structures: ACTUALS and PLAN are restricted on Unit and Version. ACT+PLAN is a calculated structure. Observe that I have no model-measure active (only default from structure)

when I remove the default I see my filters and calculations restricting already my requirements. 

 

Combine with the baseline approach. But as you can see the use is a bit different. More like a filter, not like a folder containing measures like in Query

entry.title: Fixed Price Condition in Subcontracting PO – Prevent Proration at GR
entry.content:
We have subcontracting POs where we agree with the vendor on a fixed manufacturing price. For example:

  • Agreed price to manufacture 100 units = $100

  • If at Goods Receipt (GR) the vendor delivers 99 units (due to yield/variance), the system should still keep the total agreed value of $100.

  • The system should NOT prorate the value based on the received quantity.

These POs normally:

  • Have only one line item

  • Do not allow partial deliveries

  • Are received only once

However, because this is a yield-based production scenario, sending the same quantity of components may result in 99, 100, or 101 finished units.

Additionally:

  • The component materials sent to the vendor have average price.

  • In many cases, the invoice is posted long after the GR.

  • The batch is often consumed before the actual invoice value is posted, which creates valuation inconsistencies if the GR value is prorated.

What I Tried

To fulfill the requirement, I created a new pricing condition as a copy of PBXX and adjusted some configuration settings.

The intention was to:

  • Apply a fixed total amount per PO item

  • Avoid any quantity-based proration at Goods Receipt

However, despite the changes, the system still prorates the condition value based on the received quantity.

Question

Has anyone successfully configured a pricing condition in a subcontracting PO that:

  • Applies a fixed total amount at item level

  • Does not prorate at GR based on actual received quantity

  • Keeps the full agreed amount even if GR quantity differs from PO quantity

If so:

Any guidance on the correct configuration approach would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Why SELECT returns different rows when querying a CDS View on MSEG versus MSEG directly
entry.content:
• Introduction
  • Custom CDS view ZCDS_E_MSEG
  • ABAP SELECT on MSEG
  • ABAP SELECT on ZCDS_E_MSEG
  • Why the Row Count Differs
  • Key Takeaways

 

Introduction

If you’ve recently worked with S/4HANA and created a custom CDS view on a standard table such as MSEG (*), you may have noticed something surprising:

Even if the CDS view is a simple SELECT FROM MSEG without any WHERE clause and there's no CDS Access Control implied, the number of rows returned by ABAP SELECT * FROM ZCDS_E_VIEW may not match the results of ABAP SELECT * FROM MSEG.

(*) You may find other tables like MSEG in the note 2206980 - Material Inventory Managment: change of data model in S/4HANA - SAP for Me.

 

Custom CDS view ZCDS_E_MSEG

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_V_MSEG'
define view ZCDS_E_MSEG as select from MSEG
{
  *
}

NB: a DDIC-based CDS view is used here to make the example simple, but as it's obsolete since ABAP 7.57 CDS view entities should be used instead.

 

ABAP SELECT on MSEG

SELECT * FROM MSEG
WHERE mblnr = '5000000000'
INTO TABLE (lt_mseg).
This example assumes there are some lines in MSEG for MBLNR = '5000000000'.
This ABAP SELECT may return more rows than the below ABAP SELECT on ZCDS_E_MSEG.
 

ABAP SELECT on ZCDS_E_MSEG

SELECT * FROM zcds_e_mseg
WHERE mblnr = '5000000000'
INTO TABLE (lt_cds_mseg).
Even though this query selects all rows from the CDS view ZCDS_E_MSEG (which is based on the physical MSEG table without any WHERE), the number of rows may be fewer than the ABAP SELECT on MSEG.
 

Why the Row Count Differs

  1. The table MSEG exists physically in the database but is no longer maintained

    • In S/4HANA, the real material document data lives in MATDOC.

    • The legacy table MSEG still exists in the database with the original ECC data, but is no more updated by S/4HANA transactions.

  2. ABAP SELECT on MSEG is redirected to the replacement object

    • When ABAP code does SELECT * FROM MSEG, SAP automatically redirects the query to the CDS replacement view NSDM_E_MSEG, which reads MATDOC.



      NB: NSDM_E_MSEG filters MATDOC using WHERE record_type = 'MDOC' but it's not implied in the problem discussed here.
      You can see in the SQL Trace (ST05) how ABAP SELECT ... FROM MSEG ... is automatically using the replacement object:

    • This redirection ensures that old programs continue to work correctly and return all material document lines, i.e. those lines migrated and updated in S/4HANA live.

  3. Custom CDS views on MSEG read the physical table directly

    • Any CDS view defined on MSEG does not use the replacement object, so it reads the actual content of the MSEG table, which are the data migrated from ECC, but are no more updated in S/4HANA.

    • If the table MSEG is empty in your system, it's because the Obsolete Data Handling tool has run (since S/4HANA 2023, it's the transaction code SODH, which has superseded ODH_DATA_PROCESSING). Reference: SAP S/4HANA Upgrade Guide, section Cleaning Up Obsolete Data After the Upgrade.

 

✅Key Takeaways

  • Do not rely on MSEG for new development; always use MATDOC or NSDM replacement views such as NSDM_E_MSEG.

  • NEVER use the old tables in your custom CDS views because they contain obsolete data.

  • After you have successfully tested and validated the upgrade results, you can clean up the obsolete data with the Obsolete Data Handling tool, to free space and to avoid the confusion and panic among developers.

entry.title: Integrating SAP Databricks SCIM API with SAP Identity Services
entry.content:
This blog explores how Databricks SCIM API works, its Users and Groups endpoints, and how to integrate it with SAP’s identity services for automated user provisioning.
 
SAP Databricks provides a SCIM 2.0 API (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) primarily at the account level to create, read, update, and delete users, groups, and service principals. This is the recommended and most up-to-date way for provisioning [especially when syncing from IdPs like SAP Identity Authentication (IAS)  with SAP Identity Provisioning Serivce (IPS) ].
This activity is purely managed by your BTP Administrator or Cloud Identity Admins or IT Admins.
Access to SAP Databricks is purely given to your Data Scientist or ML Engineer or Data Engineer.
 
Refer SAP Help Documentation : Managing SAP Databricks Users and Roles

Understanding Databricks SCIM API

What is SCIM?

SCIM 2.0 is an open standard protocol designed to simplify user identity management across cloud-based applications. It provides a RESTful API framework for creating, reading, updating, and deleting user accounts and group memberships.

Databricks SCIM Implementation

Databricks implements SCIM 2.0 to enable automated user provisioning from identity providers (IdPs). The API supports:

  • User Management: Create, read, update, and deactivate user accounts
  • Group Management: Manage group memberships and entitlements
  • Service Principals: Automate authentication for applications
  • Workspace Administration: Centralized identity governance

API Endpoint Structure:

https://<sap-databricks-instance>/api/2.0/preview/scim/v2/

Users API: Core Operations

Authentication

All SCIM API calls require authentication using a Databricks personal access token or service principal token:

Authorization: Bearer <access-token>
Content-Type: application/scim+json

How to get Service Principal Token (Recommended for Production)

Service principals are the best practice for application-to-application authentication which is needed for SAP Identity Provisioning to sync Users from SAP IAS (Identity Authentication User Store).

Step 1: Create a Service Principal

Via SAP Databricks UI:

  1. Go to Settings → Admin Console
  2. Navigate to Identity and access → Service principals
  3. Click Add service principal
  4. Fill in details:
    Name: sap-ips-integration
Application ID: (auto-generated or specify)
  5. Click Add and Go to the Service principal details which you created
  6. Click on Secrets to generate
  7. Set the days (Rotate every 90days or never)

 

Copy the Secret and Client ID which is required to make SCIM API calls.

Test Locally to check Token is generated successfully 

https://accounts.cloud.databricks.com/oidc/accounts/<accountID>/v1/token


Retrieving Users

GET /api/2.0/preview/scim/v2/Users
GET /api/2.0/preview/scim/v2/Users/{id}
GET /api/2.0/preview/scim/v2/Users?filter=userName eq "john.doe@company.com"

 Retrieving Group Memberships

GET /api/2.0/preview/scim/v2/Groups/{group-id}
GET /api/2.0/preview/scim/v2/Groups?filter=displayName eq "Data Scientists"

To Know your Databricks Account ID :

Integrate SAP Databricks with SAP Cloud Identity Services securely to automate users and groups provisioning across systems.

Refer SAP Help Documentation :  Provisioning Identities

How the integration works (at a glance)

  1. Source: SAP Identity Authentication (IAS)

    • Holds the users and groups that should access SAP Databricks.
    • Exposes identities via SCIM-compatible attributes to provisioning.

  2. Orchestration: SAP Identity Provisioning (IPS)

    • Acts as the bridge: reads from IAS and writes to SAP Databricks.
    • You define jobs, attribute mappings, filters, and schedules.
    • Supports delta sync (changes only) and dry-runs.

  3. Target: SAP Databricks (SCIM 2.0)

    • Receives Users and Groups via SCIM 2.0 REST API.
    • Creates/updates accounts, group memberships, entitlements (e.g., allow-cluster-create, workspace-access).
    • Deprovisions on disable/delete (soft-delete vs. hard-delete per IPS config).

Once the User Sync is successful, SAP Databricks Admin can see in users who have got access with approriate Role assigned.

entry.title: Debugging AMDP through centralized Firefighter from GRC
entry.content:
Our SAP system is on HANA 2023, and we have developed many AMDPs to meet our business requirements. The issue we are facing involves debugging AMDPs in the Production environment. Since AMDP debugging is supported only through Eclipse (ADT) and not through SAP GUI, it becomes a challenge.

As per our process, we have centralized Firefighter access. We log on to the GRC Production environment and then access the target system using transaction code GRAC_EAM, which opens only in the SAP GUI. In short, because the Firefighter session only provides GUI access, we are unable to debug AMDPs in Production.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026
entry.content:
Um Pouco da Nossa História

O SAP Inside Track teve sua primeira edição em Campinas em 2017 e, desde então, tem sido um marco importante para a comunidade SAP na região. O evento faz parte de uma série global presente em muitos países, sendo organizado por membros ativos da Comunidade SAP com o intuito de promover o compartilhamento de conhecimento e fortalecer conexões profissionais.

No Brasil, o SAP Inside Track já acumula mais de 15 anos de existência, passando por 11 cidades diferentes em 40 edições pelo país. Cidades como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, São Leopoldo, Joinville, Campinas, Salvador, Ribeirão Preto, Belo Horizonte, Curitiba, Fortaleza e  Recife.

O Que Esperar do Evento

O SAP Inside Track Campinas é a maior oportunidade de aprender, compartilhar experiências e conhecer outros profissionais do mercado SAP. Teremos palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, abordando as mais diversas soluções SAP, incluindo as inovações do SAP S/4HANA.

Além disso, este é o momento perfeito para expandir seu networking e conhecer pessoalmente grandes profissionais da Comunidade SAP e das redes sociais.

Ingressos à venda no link: https://www.sympla.com.br/evento/sap-inside-track-campinas/3307956

Voluntarios:   @RobertaFernande   @antonia_zorn  @denner_andrade8554 @Marssel700  @Secco

entry.title: Increase business outcomes by unleashing the power of AI agents
entry.content:
Co-Authors: Alessandro Biagi, Trinidad MARTINEZ GEA, Thiago Mendes, MostafaSharaf, Yatsea Li

Recently, we have kick-started a webinar series of Reimagine your customer’s business processes with AI Agents with agenda as below:

TitleSchedule
Session 1 - Increase business outcomes by unleashing the power of AI agentsCompleted. Recording and presentation available here.
Session 2 – From Conversational to Autonomous AI Agents

5-6 May 2026.  Register

Session 3 – Take your AI Agents to the next level 7-9 July 2026. Register

In the first webinar Increase business outcomes by unleashing the power of AI agents, we have showcased a prototype use case featuring a custom AI agent (Escalation Assistant) developed with Joule Studio Agent Builder, which is designed to support a Support Manager in IT Consultancy industry by streamlining the escalation-to-resolution process. In this blog post, we would like to recap the webinar and walk you through its end-to-end implementation journey. 

Let's start with some background introduction about AI Agent in general and SAP context. 

Brief Overview

AI Agent or Agentic AI

Nowadays,  AI Agent or Agentic AI is really a hit. You may have heard of Agentic Workflow, Agentic Automation, Agentic AI or AI Agent etc. in enterprise context. In the software development, Agentic Coding or Coding Agent may help you to do much of coding on your behalf with your directing.

So, what does agentic really mean? Let us understand its fundamental concept with some examples.

  • Agentic workflows are AI-driven processes that use autonomous agents to complete complex tasks with minimal human oversight. For example, an AI agent (customer support assistant) receives a customer complaint, analyzes sentiment, retrieves order data from ERP,  research and proposes a resolution, drafts a response email, and logs the case—end to end.
  • Agentic automation is the use of AI agents to perform tasks autonomously, going beyond traditional automation's rule-based execution. One example in procurement-to-pay, a procurement agent monitors supply chain risks, checking procurement policy, compares vendors, negotiates via email, and creates purchase orders based on real-time conditions.
  • Agentic AI refers to autonomous artificial intelligence systems that can act independently to achieve goals, using planning, reasoning, and learning to execute tasks without constant human oversight. Think of a financial planning agent that analyses cash flow trends, runs forecasting models, simulates scenarios, and recommends budget adjustments.

Okay, how are these Agentic systems or AI Agents different from the traditional workflow, robotics process automation(RPA), traditional AI?

Traditional workflows and RPA follow fixed rules. Traditional AI predicts or classifies.

Agentic systems or AI Agents, by contrast, exhibit a higher level of autonomy by leveraging state-of-art LLM's capabilities in planning, reasoning, reflection, and tool usage (e.g., APIs, databases) as illustrated in the diagram below.

In short, traditional automation executes tasks. Agentic AI pursues goals. And the shift from task execution to goal-driven autonomy unlocks new business outcomes.

 

Joule Skill and AI Agent

Let's zoom into SAP context about building custom AI Agent. There are three approaches featured as below. 

Specifically, we'll focus on Joule Studio in this blog post, which enables you to build custom Joule Skills and AI Agents through low-code/no-code approach.

So, what are the differences between Joule Skills and AI Agents?

In short, Skills follow the notion of “Tell me exactly what to do, and I’ll execute it",  while AI Agents operate on the principle of “Tell me the problem, and I’ll determine the best way to solve it.” 

Joule Skills are rule-based and deterministic. You define the workflow, the sequence of steps, validations, and system calls in advance. The problem and the solution path are both clearly specified. Skills can orchestrate and integrate with APIs, processes, and automations etc. with nature language understanding capability.

They are ideal for structured, repeatable tasks that require reliability and control.

Examples of Joule Skills:

  • A skill for Invoice creation in SAP HANA Cloud: Creating an invoice in SAP S/4HANA Cloud based on details provided by the user in natural language and performing some predefined checks.
  • A skill for Vendor Selection in procurement involving multiple system: Fetching detailed vendor data, purchase request, RFQ information, and supply chain risk data from multiple systems e.g. SAP Ariba and 3rd party system through API, then feeding into the vendor screening process defined as a decision table in SAP Build Process Automation in combination of price, delivery , and risk level etc for vendor selection. 
  • ...

On the other hand, AI Agents handle complex or ambiguous tasks where the solution path is not predefined or just too many solution paths to be explicitly defined. They break problems into manageable subtasks and decide dynamically what to do next using advanced planning and reasoning, mimicking human problem-solving with the help of LLMs.

Agents can autonomously use tools like Joule Skill, Document Grounding, Calculator, and Model Context Protocol(MCP) Servers or Sub-agents for collaboration. 

Examples of AI Agents:

  • A procurement advisor agent:  Autonomously retrieving purchase request and vendor data,  assessing supply chain risk with public data, checking procurement policy and vendor contract,  recommending vendor and negotiating with vendor via email with human supervision etc.

  • A customer escalation assistant: The agent retrieves the support incident associated to an escalation request, checks escalation policy,  proposes the possible solutions by searching web and internal knowledge base, and drafts customer communication.

  • ...

Next, let's move on to a practical use case of custom AI Agent(escalation assistant) with Joule Studio for Escalation Management in customer service, and see how the escalation assistant help to streamline the process of escalation-to-resolution with Agentic AI.

 

Use Case - Escalation Assistant

Emma, a support manager at an IT consultancy company, manages customer escalations to ensure customer satisfaction and continuous process improvement. Therefore, the essential need is to expedite escalation to resolution and provide responsive customer communication.

The Traditional Workflow of  Escalation Management

Let's examine Emma's usual routine tasks of handling an escalation request without any help of Escalation Assistant. 

  1. Jane from the customer, has been frustratedly waiting for a resolution to their support incident, which has been reported 30 days ago, now have significant impact on their daily business. She sends an escalation request about to Emma through e-mail.
  2. Upon receiving the escalation request, Emma first needs to check and qualify the associated incident to the escalation request against the company’s escalation policy.
  3. If qualified, Emma drafts and sends a quick response email to reassure the customer. Otherwise, reply the customer about the rejection of the escalation with clear explanation. 
  4. As part of the escalation policy, if the incident priority is low or medium, she can increase it to high and notify the responsible support consultant and the customer about the escalation.
  5. Emma attempts to find a solution by searching the web and internal knowledge base about the relevant topic and respond the customer with the solution via an email.
  6. ... 

In a traditional escalation management workflow, Emma has to manually manage multiple touchpoints including customer interactions, escalation requests from various channels, the ticketing system, company escalation policy and support consultants etc. This process is trivia and time-consuming. Next, let's see how we can help Emma to automate and streamline the process of escalation to resolution with AI Agent.

Agentic Automation of Escalation Management

Instead of manually performing these tasks, Emma asks help from the Escalation Assistant (a custom AI Agent built with Joule Studio Agent Builder), which automates  the end-to-end process on her behalf.

Here list the high-level the capabilities of AI Agent in Joule Studio mapping with Emma's manual tasks in the workflow of escalation management.

  1.  Escalation policy is checked against the ticket using document grounding.
  2. Web search of issue topic through MCP(Model Context Protocol) servers.
  3. Knowledge base search about the similar issue are handled through Joule skills and actions.
  4. Retrieving and updating Incident ticket are handled through Joule skills and actions.
  5. The email communication about status and  the solution is sent by a process automation within a Joule skill.

Final End-to-End Demo

Let's have a look at the end-to-end demo to see how Escalation Assistant can help Emma to streamline and automate the escalation-to-resolution process.

 

Design of Escalation Assistant

Now that we have an idea about the use case. Let's move to the design of the agent. We'll convert the process into an agentic diagram, which illustrate the design of the multiple-agent systems and how each agent collaborate together towards the end goal.

Agentic Diagram 

The design of Escalation Assistant follow the principle of modular multiple agent system. Each AI Agent is specialised in a specific task. The root agent Escalation Assistant act as an orchestrator, responsible for orchestrating the other agents in collaborating together towards the end goals.
Here list the agents of Escalation Assistant. Click each item to find out its configurations in Joule Studio, such as expertise, instructions etc. which can be replicated in your own Joule Studio.

AI AgentResponsibilityRoleUsed by
Escalation AssistantOrchestrating the other three sub agentsRoot AgentEntry Point
Escalation AdvisorChecking company policySub AgentEscalation Assistant
Advisory ExpertProviding a straightforward solution by leveraging another two sub agents: the Web Searcher and Knowledge Base SearcherSub AgentEscalation Assistant
Web SearcherSearching the web with the generated querySub AgentAdvisory Expert
Knowledge Base SearcherSearching the knowledge base with the generated querySub AgentAdvisory Expert
Service Call HandlerRetrieving and updating service callsRoot AgentEscalation Assistant

 

Solution Architecture

If we look at the solution architecture of the whole solution, we can see how  our Joule Skills integrate seamlessly with various systems via destinations.

We use SAP Field Service Management for handling Service Calls in the PoC, that said, it could be SAP Service Cloud or SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution etc. with ticketing capability.

It connects our Smart Advisory Companion back-end application for searching internal knowledge base, which is built in Python and deployed on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime. The back-end leverages several powerful SAP BTP capabilities, including: Generative AI Hub for accessing and utilising Large Language Models properly grounded with the appropriate business context.

The Joule Skills will serve as tools for the Joule Agents. Both are implemented using Joule Studio in SAP Build and deployed directly into the Joule tenant within SAP BTP.

The agents will also rely on some MCP Servers to accomplish their job, such as web search.

With each user interaction through the Joule Application Client, Joule intelligently selects the most relevant agent to execute, based on their descriptions, ensuring an efficient and contextual response every time.

 

End-to-End Implementation Journey

By now we have gone through the use case and technical design, let's dive into the end-to-end implementation journey of Escalation Assistant with Joule Studio Agent Builder.

1. Reusable Joule Skills

Before going into the implementation details of the AI agents from the agentic diagram, let’s just have a quick overview on some Joule Skills reused in multiple agents.

  • Execute Request: A generic Joule Skill as API Gateway to execute any OData or RESTful API, which is used by Service Call Handler for reading and writing Service Calls.
  • Send Email: A reusable Joule Skill to send email with dynamic input parameters such as email title, recipient, message body etc.

2. Service Call Handler

Let's start with the implementation of Service Call Handler agent which is responsible for reading and writing service calls in SAP Field Service Management.

Demo Recording:

 

3.Escalation Advisor

The retrieved service call information by Service Call Handler Agent is handed over to another agent Escalation Advisor for checking the service call against the company's escalation policy to see if its associated escalation request is qualified as an escalation. Let’s see its configuration. 

 

4. Advisory Expert

Once the escalation request is qualified as an escalation, then the Advisory Expert agent will work on some possible solution proposal to the escalated issue by searching the web and internal knowledge base about the related topic about the escalation though another two sub-agents Web Searcher and Knowledge Base Searcher, then crafting a solution based on the search results. Let's check out  its implementation.

 

5. Escalation Assistant

Finally, the Escalation Assistant orchestrates and chains the sub agents to collaborate together throughout the end-to-end process of escalation handling. Let's examine its configuration details.

 

6. Testing

Once the agents are configured, let's test the Escalation Assistant to examine its execution detail with built-in testing tool of Joule Studio. It may take multiple iterations of tuning and testing of agent configurations until it yields expected outcome.

We have just completed the end-to-end implementation journey of Escalation Assistant. Let's review some best practices.

Best Practices of Creating Joule Agents

  • Be clear and specific: Define a clear goal and the steps to reach it.

  • Use step-by-step logic: Break complex rules into simple, ordered lists.

  • Provide actionable instructions: Ensure every step leads to a clear action (usually a tool call).

  • Avoid conflicting rules: Establish a clear priority for rules to prevent confusion.

  • Restrict the toolset: Grant access only to the tools essential for the agent's job.

  • Use English for all design: Write all core instructions and tool descriptions in English for consistency.

  • Follow the design principle of modularity and reusability: Break down the complex use case into multiple modular agents. Each sub-agent is specialised in a single role with clear and specific instructions. Orchestrate the multiple sub agents to collaborate towards the goal.  

  • Test throughout with different configuration: Test with different LLMs and agent configurations to find out the best-fit. 

More details about the best practices available here.

 

Additional Resources 

In addition, here you can learn more resoures how to design a Joule agents in Joule Studio.

  • Learning Journey: Introducing Joule Studio in SAP Build
  • Developer Tutorial: Use Joule Studio to Create an Agent
  • Discovery Center: Build custom AI Agents with Joule Studio
  • Check out our Github Repository of Escalation Assistant
  • Review our Webinar Recoding: Increase business outcomes by unleashing the power of AI agents

 

Wrap up

In this blog post, we have learned the overview of AI Agent, Joule Skill and Joule Agent. Joule skill is ideal for structured, repeatable tasks that require reliability and control. And Joule Agent handle complex or ambiguous tasks where the solution path is not predefined or too many solution paths to be explicitly described. In addition, we have also gone through the end-to-end journey of implementing an Joule Agent about Escalation Assistant.  If you would like to learn more about custom AI Agent,  please register to our webinars about Reimagine your customer’s business processes with AI Agents.

entry.title: EarlyWatch Alert アーカイブ
entry.content:
Solution Manager のデータベースの容量が増加しています。

お客様のSolution Manager は、EWAにのみ利用されているため、EWAのデータ容量が増加していると推測できます。

データ容量の削減について検討しており、以下のノートを参照しましたが、不明点があるので質問します。

2075483 - EarlyWatch Alert Archiving - SAP for Me

1.以下のステップに関して、Solution Manager 7.2の場合はどのような設定方法になりますか？リンク先は3.2 or 7.0の記載のみでした。

Main Steps

First of all you need to decide whether you want to archive service sessions or you want to delete them directly. Setting up your archiving is explained in 546685 note point 1: "Necessary settings in Customizing".
2.以下のステップに関して、データ削除としてレポート'RDSMOPREDUCEDATA 'を週次ジョブとして実行するにあたって、アーカイブされたデータは、レポート'RDSMOPREDUCEDATA 'による削除後も参照でき、DB容量も削減されるという理解でよいでしょうか。また、アーカイブデータの削除は'RDARCH_ARCHIVE_DELETE_SOLMAN 'でのみ削除できるという理解でよいでしょうか。

Main Steps

5.The recommended report for deleting service sessions,.....


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: EarlyWatch Alert アーカイブ
entry.content:
ご担当者様

お世話になっております。
ご質問につき、下記回答させていただきます。

質問＞1.以下のステップに関して、Solution Manager 7.2の場合はどのような設定方法になりますか？リンク先は3.2 or 7.0の記載のみでした。
…
＝＞Solution Manager 7.2の場合では、SAPノート2661928をご確認いただければ。

質問＞2.以下のステップに関して、データ削除としてレポート'RDSMOPREDUCEDATA 'を週次ジョブとして実行するにあたって、アーカイブされたデータは、レポート'RDSMOPREDUCEDATA 'による削除後も参照でき、DB容量も削減されるという理解でよいでしょうか。
＝＞はい、ご認識の通りです。レポートRDSMOPREDUCEDATAの利用は、データスペースを解放できます。

>また、アーカイブデータの削除は'RDARCH_ARCHIVE_DELETE_SOLMAN 'でのみ削除できるという理解でよいでしょうか。
=>'RDARCH_ARCHIVE_DELETE_SOLMANのほかには、レポートRDSMOPREDUCEDATAもアーカイブされたデータの削除にも使えます。
関連KBA：　2427041 - How to delete old EWA sessions according to retention times automatically through a jobの「Need to know」部分をご確認ください。

以上、ご確認のほどよろしくお願いいたします。
SAPサポート

entry.title: How-to: Mobile onboarding of SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
With so much happening 'on the floor' of any logistics operations, it is only normal that you would want to empower your employees and mobilise them, so that they can report on their warehouse activities in real time, as it happens. True to this belief, SAP Logistics Management can be used in conjunction with a readily available native app (available for Apple and Android platforms) to work hand in hand with its cloud backend to perform certain warehouse tasks. There is also a beautiful Fiori packing app that can be used on a say, a tablet, but that will be the subject of another blog.

In this blog we will cover the onboarding process of mobile devices (an Apple iPhone to be specific). We will see what an administrator must do to educate users in onboarding their device, and round off with an example showing what the onboarding process looks like.

As a foreword note, I would also like to reference the SAP help which discusses the -Mobile- Onboarding process , which I encourage you to bookmark and check for updates regularly. Also, in this blog I will cover the onboarding process aided by the scanning of a QR code. Note that onboarding can also be performed via MDM (Mobile Device Management), but I would refer you to the SAP help, if this scenario applied to you.

 

Administrator Responsibilities

As an administrator, your role will entail several tasks:

  • Having the right roles assigned to you in Cloud Identity Services (CIS) to be able to access Mobile Services
  • Get acquainted with the Mobile Services environment if this is new to you
  • Making sure your users download/access the right application
  • Providing your users with the QR code to scan to onboard their mobile device
  • Providing your users with a credentials that will allow them to authenticate in the app (the same one as if they accessed SAP Logistics Management via a browser on a desktop or tablet). This is considered standard user onboarding and not covered here.
  • Setting a client policy (including the security policy). This will not be covered here, but the SAP help as an expansive section on this.
  • Providing your users with a 'Getting Started' information pack so they know what they need to do.
  • Regularly checking that the devices registered in mobile services are still active and legitimate devices.

Having the right access to Mobile Services

As an administrator, to access the Mobile Services, you need the right access. For this first part, obtaining the QR code, you can get away with a display role, but you will need an administrator role, if you do any maintenance in mobile services (eg removing devices, setting security policies, etc...).

In my case I obtained the admin role (SAPMS_Administrator). The roles are described in the SAP help in this page.

Accessing Mobile Services

This section is described in the SAP help here. I bring your attention to this page, as you need to perform a search and replace, within a URL to access to the mobile services. In this page, it states that you must replace the <subdomain> in the URL provided, with your subdomain (note that the URL to use will vary based on the data centre where you deployed SAP Logistics Management - the reference links to use are indicated in this section of the SAP help)

https://<subdomain>.lgm-mobile.cfapps.eu20-001.hana.ondemand.com/mobileservices/Admin/index.html 

In case this is unclear, the subdomain is the BTP subaccount where you deployed SAP Logistics Management (LGM henceforth). If you are unsure what that is (which could also be incorrect if you are using SAP Build Work Zone, and deployed in a different subaccount ), then you can obtain this from the LGM local launchpad URL (if unsure here, then go to your BTP subaccount where LGM was deployed, find the application named 'SAP Logistics Management' in 'Instances and Subscriptions' and click the link to open it. Once authenticated, you will see the URL you need).

In my case it is as below:

 Now that I can assemble all the parts of the URL, I can point my browser to mobile services.

 Once there, you want to navigate to the 'API' tab, and this should expose for you the QR code, you need to share with your users (you can screenshot it, save it as an image..)

 Obtaining the Mobile App

The companion app for LGM is available on the App stores of both Apple and Google, and the download links are provided below.

Apple App Store
Google Play Store

Onboarding your users

Onboarding your users, should be quite straight forward : Download an app, scan a QR code, enter credentials, set a default warehouse, work!

However, since every organisation may have a different spin on this process, which is quite flexible (provide devices with apps pre-loaded, different security policies...), I am below adding a Powerpoint that captures the main steps of the default process. You can also if you wish freely use this document as an accelerator and tailor it to your own context before providing it to your users.

I am also adding a video recording of this onboarding process that you can also check out on YouTube.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate values in inventory ADSO with non-cumulatives
entry.content:
Hi, Oscar

I hope your issue was resolve after such a long time, but if not, there could be some clue from my side.

I assume that your ADSOA is inventory enabled and ADSO B is a standard one but not marked as 'Inventory Enable',

In the meantime, DTP (with full mode) from A to B will extract from both /BIC/A'DSOA'1 (inbound table), /BIC/A'DSOA'2( active table), and, last but not least, /BIC/A'DSOA'5 (reference point table), which could be the root cause for duplicate value.

For double confirm, you can check both data records from these 3 tables (or you can directly go to view /BIC/A'DSOA'6, this is a view that union from table1, 2 and 5  ), and sum the stock value, you might find the summation value should be $691.78.

About why the behavior like this, you can refer to this SAP Note for more feature about inventory, if you want to only have a quick glance, you can go to the paragraph [IX] in this article--->1548125 - Interesting Facts about Non-Cumulatives - SAP for Me

Seems in most of the SAP's document they only consider the target ADSO(DSO B in your case) as the inventory enable one, in such case, source records in different table will be moved to corresponding target table automatically and without error.

But from the situation in fact, the target could be very flexible use, or limited by scenario, for example, I do have to use a standard ADSO as a target.

In this case, you have to manipulate field '0recordtp' case by case, to make sure the target DSO to have correct value.

Here is my solution from experience, you can add one code at start routine, Like 'Delete source_package where 0RECORDTP = 1'

This will make sure all record from reference point table will be deleted without move to target.

Hope that will be helpful.

 

BR

Ran

entry.title: SAP Developer News, February 26th, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS

March Developer Challenge Announcement - ABAP 

  • Application Development and Automation Blog Posts on the SAP Community https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/bg-p/application-developmentblog-board
  • Learn about the SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/joule-for-developers-abap-ai-capabilities-f14ebffef77b41bfb0746c33dcb70e84 

Implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in an SAP Environment 

  • Implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in an SAP Environment: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-knowledge-base/implementing-retrieval-augmented-generation-rag-in-an-sap-environment/ta-p/14325588  
  • SAP CodeJam Topics: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407#codejam1  
  • SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 - Brazil edition: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-roadshow-2026-brazil-edition-kicking-off-in-march-and/ba-p/14317926

SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2602 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2602/ba-p/14329182 

Integration Suite updates 

  • What's new in SAP Cloud Integration https://help.sap.com/whats-new/cf0cb2cb149647329b5d02aa96303f56?locale=en-US&lastSent=2026-02-16&Component=SAP+Cloud+Application+Event+Hub;Integration%2BSuite%2BGeneral%2BFeatures;Event%2BMesh;Edge%2BIntegration%2BCell;Cloud%2BIntegration;API%2BManagement&Valid_as_Of=2026-01-01:2026-03-31  

SAP Audit Log Viewer for Global Accounts and SAP Audit Log Viewer Now Automatically Enabled for All Subaccounts 

  • SAP Audit Log Viewer service for the Cloud Foundry Environment https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/audit-log-viewer-for-cloud-foundry-environment  

DYK #5 - Search ABAP CDS Objects using Annotations 

  • ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse guide https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/about-abap-development-tools-user-guide  

CHAPTER TITLES

00:00 Intro 
00:07 March Developer Challenge Announcement-ABAP 
01:25 Implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in an SAP Environment 
03:23 SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2602 
04:42 Integration Suite updates 
05:35 SAP Audit Log Viewer for Global Accounts and SAP Audit Log Viewer Now Automatically Enabled for All Subaccounts 
06:30 DYK #5 - Search ABAP CDS Objects using Annotations 

TRANSCRIPT

This is the SAP Developer News for February 26th, 2026.

[Mamikee]
Hey everyone, we're kicking off this year's SAP Developer Challenge with a topic that's very close to home, ABAP. With AI dominating conversations across the tech world, we thought this month would be the perfect time for our community to explore Joule for Developers. SAP Joule for Developers brings embedded AI capabilities to SAP Build and ABAP, helping you accelerate app and extension development, reduce development costs, and boost overall developer productivity. Now, for this challenge, we'll be using the basic trial offering for SAP Build, which includes an SAP BTP ABAP environment with Joule for Developers. Here's how it will work. So in week one, we'll focus on setting up your ABAP instance from the basic trial in ADT and start exploring some of Joule' key capabilities as the weeks go on. The challenge will run for four weeks starting Monday, March 2nd and ending March 23rd. But don't worry, we'll keep submissions open until the end of the month for anyone running a little behind. Now, to count toward the overall monthly challenge, make sure you complete and submit each week's task. Week 1 drops on the SAP Community on Monday, March 2nd, so get ready, and we can't wait to see what you build.

[Witalij]
At the recent SAP Now AI event I attended, most of our customers showcased their AI implementations using the Retrieval Augmented Generation, or RAG, approach. RAG grounds generative AI applications in private business data, delivering accurate and trustworthy responses. SAP Business Technology Platform supports three RAG patterns, as outlined by my friend Lyuba Nakryyko from the BTP Center of Excellence. First, custom RAG with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine. Best suited for structured text, such as product descriptions or service notes. This method converts data into semantic embeddings within SAP HANA Cloud, enabling context-aware responses from large language models. Second, Knowledge Graph Engine in SAP HANA Cloud is optimized for structured data with complex relationships, such as supply chains or bills of materials. It maps entities and their relationships, translates queries into precise data retrieval operations, and feeds relevant facts to the language model. Third, Managed Document Grounding, a turnkey solution for document-based Q&A, for example on PDFs, in SAP AI Core. It automates document processing and retrieval, integrating smoothly with SAP Joule or Generative AI Hub for rapid deployment. Each pattern caters to different data types and structured text, structured relationships, or document repositories, enabling you to choose the approach that best fits your business and technical needs. Lyuba's article on SAP Community is linked in the description. Also in the description, you will find a link to SAP CodeJam topics, which explore hands-on opportunities covering these different approaches in SAP Business Technology Platform. The closest SAP CodeJam events will be held in Brazil in April, and sign -ups are already open. Sim, amigos. Estamos a caminho. Até logo.

[Rich]
Hey, folks. Rich Heilman here. Long time. No see. I had to bribe Sheena and Shilpa this week to allow me to do the news piece, so here goes. That's right, folks, it's that time again. SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2602 has been released with more ABAP goodness for all of our ABAP developers out there. Nora Klemp from ABAP Product Management has posted a blog post outlining all of the new features. Some notable new features include Collaborative Draft, which allows developers to create applications which leverage draft capabilities across business objects. Recommendations is another cool feature that enhances the dropdown list or value helps with recommendations on what the value could or should be based on context and some AI magic. Lots of other new features as well, including a new My Home Page as the landing page for ABAP systems, which will have additional features added in over time. For more information on all this ABAP good stuff, please check out the link to Nora's blog post and get all the details today. See you next time, friends. Bye for now.

[Antonio]
Hola, SAP Developers. As usual, many things happening in the integration space. And I just want to share with you some of the highlights in case you missed them. In Cloud Integration, there are many new adapters. Microsoft Outlook, Zendesk Receiver Adapter, Bamboo HR Receiver Adapter. There's also a new version of the Shopify Adapter that supports OAuth 2 client credentials. Related to Groovy Scripts, you can now upload Groovy Script 1.x and 2.0 in the Script Collection artifact and in the integration flow. When you optimize Groovy scripts with AI, you can now get a review report and download the original script. In API Management, we can now discover and publish events from Advanced Event Mesh in Developer Hub. I'm including the relevant links in the description, so make sure to check them out. Hasta luego!

[DJ]
It's always worth keeping an eye on the What's New for SAP Business Technology Platform facility on the SAP Help Portal for upcoming features and releases. Something that will likely be of particular interest for us all is the very recent general availability of the SAP Audit Log Viewer for SAP BTP in the cockpit. It's available for audit logs at the global account, so you can look at entitlements and assignments made, for example, And also, you can look at the creation and deletion of subaccounts. And talking of subaccounts, it's also available for all subaccounts too. You don't need any entitlement or subscription to the corresponding service to use it. Check out the documentation. Links in the description.

[Shilpa]
Did you know you can search the CDS View entity by the annotations used? Choose the annotation. Now, this is the annotation indicating extensibility enabled. Choose Get Various List from the context. Choose the required object types, apply filter to restrict the search. Use control space to get the options. And click Finish. The Various List is opened in the search view and displays the search result as a list. Similarly, you can also choose the value of annotation and repeat the same. Here I have selected the authorization check annotation with value as check. So these are the CDS views which has the authorization marked as required. Not just the annotation, you can also search the usage of view entity in the similar way. Choose the view entity and select get where used list. Now this result gives the list of CDS view entity where it has been used.
